results.title: Growing girls
results.brief_description: Aims at instructing pre-adolescent and adolescent girls in the physiology and hygiene of menstruation, emphasizing the normality of the process. The story is woven around 13-year old Mary and is introduced by showing her and her friend on the school playing field. Photographic shots are used to show how all young creatures grow up - chickens, calves, tadpoles, and humans. Animated diagrams illustrate the physiology of menstruation and the development of the human foetus. Photography is then used to demonstrate the hygiene of menstruation and the reasonable precautions necessary while menstruating.

results.title: Growing dollars
results.creator_credit: Tina Viljoen, Barrie Howells and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: The dramatic increase in grain production during the ‘Green Revolution’ in the mid-1960’s, created an important agricultural advance in many Third World countries. However, it had wide repercussions on all types of farming. Large scale mechanised farms began to take over smaller farms, forcing small and tenant farmers who lost their land to migrate to the cities, where they swelled the ranks of the urban unemployed. This documentary is a look at ‘agri-business’, a term to describe the multi-national corporations that are attempting to create a world agricultural system in which everything they produce is for customers in the already wealthy countries. (Awards: Berlin)

results.title: Group productivity (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Ron Underwood, Janice M Reynolds and CRM Productions
results.brief_description: Divides group development into three phases: Orientation, Power Distribution and Tasking, Team Production and Feedback. Demonstrates the delicate balance between tasking and nurturing for each phase and gives examples of coping and motivational strategies useful in daily living.

results.title: Groundwater
results.creator_credit: Bert Van Bork and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: Explains the origins of groundwater and the relation of groundwater to the water cycle. Emphasizes the need for conservation and protection of the water supply from chemical and nuclear waste.

results.title: Groove squad [DVD]
results.brief_description: In this animated feature, three exuberant teenage friends, Chrissie, Ping and Mac, are ambitious cheerleaders and students at Bay City High School. After a freak accident, and much to their delight, they are transformed into super-heroes obtaining the skills, respectively, of extraordinary strength, X-ray vision and flying. Joining the dynamic trio in their quest to combat the evil-doings of various villains is Adrian, their funky hairdresser and Zeke, a boy who nurses a serious crush on Ping. The girls however don’t neglect their cheerleading duties but continue to practise their rigorous routines in readiness for the Bay City cheerleading championships. As head cheerleader, Chrissie is frequently in conflict with her colleague, Star, a wealthy girl who covets Chrissie’s status. As the Groove squad learn to comprehend the extent of their powers (which come and go, not always at their behest) they learn the value of working as a team in the spirit of friendship. This film, suitable for children and pitched at teens and pre-teens, has an endearing comic tone that supports the neo-feminist values of ‘girl power’.

results.title: Grizzly
results.creator_credit: Jeff Turner, Patrick Morris, Sue Turner, Neil Nightingale, BBC and Discovery Channel
results.brief_description: The Grizzly bear is the world’s largest carnivore. Weighing up to 1,000 lbs, they can run as fast as a racehorse. They’re smart, they’re resourceful and they’re very dangerous. Presented by David Attenborough, this video is the story of Jeff Turner, a nature cameraman who has spent the best part of the last 15 years filming Grizzly bears in their natural state in the coastal rainforests of western Canada. Because he understands the nature of the bears so well, he is able to get closer to them than ever before and it is this intimate footage which makes this documentary so special.

results.title: Gristle
results.creator_credit: Andrew Murray, Carolyn Johnson, Carolyn-Johnson Films, Ernest Films and New South Wales Film and Television Office
results.brief_description: Two animal transport workers from the company Flying Animals (Rhys Muldoon and Darren Gilshenan) find themselves in possession of a dead cat called Gristle - a certain blot on Doug’s perfect record which could impede future promotions.
